BasicAuthOnBranches.^description: Enabling basic authentication on Amplify branches restricts access to your preview and staging environments. Without basic auth, anyone with the branch URL can access your application deployments, potentially exposing unreleased features, sensitive data, or development environments to unauthorized users.
BasicAuthOnBranches.shortDesc: Enable basic auth on Amplify branches

CustomDomainConfigured.^description: Configuring a custom domain for your Amplify application provides a professional, branded URL instead of the default amplifyapp.com domain. Custom domains also enable you to configure custom SSL certificates and DNS settings for your application.

AccessLogsEnabled.^description: Access logging for AWS Amplify captures information about requests made to your application. These logs are valuable for security auditing, troubleshooting access issues, and analyzing usage patterns. Without access logs, you lose visibility into who is accessing your application.

BranchAutoBuildEnabled.^description: Enabling auto-build on Amplify branches ensures that your application is automatically built and deployed whenever new code is pushed to the connected repository. Without auto-build, deployments must be triggered manually, increasing the risk of deployment delays and inconsistencies.

WAFAssociation.^description: AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall) protects your Amplify application from common web exploits and bots that could affect availability, compromise security, or consume excessive resources. Without WAF, your application is exposed to SQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), and other OWASP Top 10 threats.
WAFAssociation.shortDesc: Associate WAF Web ACL with Amplify app
